articles.headline: Nvidia’s spending $4 billion on photonics to stay ahead of the curve in AI
articles.description: Nvidia announced on Monday that it's investing $2 billion each into Lumentum and Coherent, which are both developing photonics technology for data centers, like optical transceivers, circuit switches, and lasers, which are used to move data at high speeds over long distances. Their tech could improve energy efficiency, data transfer speeds, and bandwidth in future […]

articles.headline: I’m on the Meta Oversight Board. We need AI protections now | Suzanne Nossel
articles.description: AI is transforming our world. Accepting independent oversight is the least companies can do to protect our rightsThe speed with which AI is transforming our lives is head-spinning. Unlike previous technological revolutions – radio, nuclear fission or the internet – governments are not leading the way. We know that AI can be dangerous; chatbots advise teens on suicide and may soon be capable of instructing on how to create biological weapons. Yet there is no equivalent to the Federal Drug Administration, testing new models for safety before public release. Unlike in the nuclear industry, companies often don’t have to disclose dangerous breaches or accidents. The tech industry’s lobbying muscle, Washington’s paralyzing polarization, and the sheer complexity of such a potent, fast-moving technology have kept federal regulation at bay. European officials are facing pushback against rules that some claim hobble the continent’s competitiveness. Although several US states are piloting AI laws, they operate in a tentative patchwork and Donald Trump has attempted to render them invalid.Heads of AI platforms like OpenAI’s ChatGPT and Google’s Gemini say they care about safety. But owning the future of AI means pouring billions into models that not even their creators fully understand, and making choices like adding ads – and the capabilities that the Pentagon is now seeking from Anthropic – that raise risk. Anthropic, which styles itself as the most conscientious frontier AI company, says its model is trained to “imagine how a thoughtful senior Anthropic employee” would weigh helpfulness against possible harm. The directive echoes criticisms levied years ago over Silicon Valley companies that shaped the lives of users worldwide from insular boardrooms. Consumers don’t believe they are in good hands. Fully 77% of Americans surveyed last year think AI could pose a threat to humanity. Continue reading...

articles.headline: Apple launches lower cost iPhone 17e and a new iPad Air powered by its M4 chip
articles.description: Apple opens week of product launches with refreshed low-cost iPhone and a faster iPad Air, as it begins what looks to be a broader multi-day hardware push.

software_engineering.headline: Nvidia invests $4 billion in photonics for faster, more efficient AI data centers.
software_engineering.what_changed: Nvidia is investing $2 billion each into Lumentum and Coherent, companies developing photonics technology like optical transceivers and circuit switches.
software_engineering.why_care: This investment aims to improve data transfer speeds, bandwidth, and energy efficiency in future data centers, directly impacting the infrastructure your applications run on.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Research how optical networking advancements might influence your future cloud architecture and data transfer strategies.

software_engineering.headline: Meta Oversight Board member calls for urgent AI safety regulations.
software_engineering.what_changed: A Meta Oversight Board member highlighted the lack of independent oversight and regulation for AI, despite its rapid, transformative, and potentially dangerous capabilities.
software_engineering.why_care: The absence of clear AI safety standards means you might be developing or deploying AI systems without consistent guidelines, increasing risks for your projects and users.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Familiarize yourself with emerging AI ethics guidelines and responsible AI development practices to mitigate potential risks in your projects.

software_engineering.headline: Apple launches new iPhone 17e and M4-powered iPad Air.
software_engineering.what_changed: Apple released a new, lower-cost iPhone 17e and an updated iPad Air featuring its M4 chip.
software_engineering.why_care: The M4 chip in the new iPad Air indicates Apple's continued focus on powerful, efficient hardware, which could influence your mobile application development and testing strategies.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Review your application's performance on the latest Apple hardware specifications, especially if you target mobile or tablet users.
